originalTitle: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
title: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
content: Caf executive committee member Augustin Senghor says the decision to strip Senegal of the 2025 Afcon title is "unacceptable, abject and we have to denounce it".

originalTitle: South Africans say criminal gangs are exploiting the water crisis
title: Water Worries in South Africa
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, super friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter from Africa here. Today, we have an important story from a country called South Africa. South Africa is a beautiful country on our amazing continent. It's part of Africa! We are going to talk about water. Water is super important for everyone, right? We drink it, we wash with it, and our plants need it to grow. It helps us stay healthy and happy.

What's Happening with Water?

Imagine waking up and turning on your tap, but no water comes out. And this happens not just for a day, but for many, many days! This is what's happening to some families in South Africa right now. They have been without "mains water" for weeks. What is mains water? It's the water that flows from the big pipes underground, straight into your house. It's the water that usually comes out of your kitchen or bathroom taps! When it's gone for weeks, it means a very, very long time. Can you imagine a whole month without water from your tap?

Why is Water So Important?

Water is like magic! We need it for so many things every single day. We need to drink water to stay healthy and strong. Our bodies are mostly made of water! Did you know? About 60% of your body is water! That's more than half! You need to drink water to keep all your body parts working well. We also need water to wash our hands and faces, to take baths, and to clean our clothes. Imagine how tricky it would be without water for all these things! Cooks use water to make yummy food too. Farmers need water to help their crops grow so we can have food to eat. Water is truly a gift that we sometimes take for granted.

What is a "Water Crisis"?

When there's not enough water for everyone, we call it a "water crisis." It means there's a big problem with getting water to people. Sometimes, it's because there hasn't been enough rain. We call this a "drought." It means the land is very dry for a long time. Other times, the big water pipes that bring water to homes can break. Or maybe there are other problems that stop the water from flowing properly. A water crisis makes life very hard for families. It means they have to find other ways to get water every day.

Oh No! Bad Guys!

Now, here's a sad part of the story. When people are having a hard time, sometimes bad people try to take advantage. These bad people are called "criminal gangs." A gang is a group of people. And "criminal" means they do things that are wrong and against the rules. They break the law. These gangs are "exploiting" the water crisis. This means they are using the problem to do bad things and make money for themselves. They are taking advantage of people's need for water when they are already struggling.

How Are They Taking Advantage?

So, how do these bad guys take advantage? Imagine families waiting for water. Sometimes, big water trucks bring water to areas where there is none. People line up with their buckets and bottles. But these criminal gangs can come and try to steal the water. They might try to sell the water for too, too much money. This is not fair! People are already struggling, and they should not have to pay too much for something as basic as water. The gangs might even make it a bit scary for people to go and get water. They can cause trouble and make it harder for kind people to help.

What Do People Do Without Water?

When the taps are dry for weeks, people have to be very clever and strong. They might walk long distances to find a working tap. They might line up for hours to get water from special water tanks or trucks. Sometimes, kind neighbours who still have some water will share it with others. People become very good at saving every drop of water. They might use the same water for different things. For example, water used to wash rice might then be used to water plants. It's a tough way to live, but people help each other.

How Can We Be Water Heroes?

Did you know? Even if you have water at home, it's always good to be water wise! That means not wasting water. You can help by turning off the tap when you brush your teeth. You can take shorter showers. You can make sure no taps are dripping. Every drop saved helps! When we save water, it means there is more for everyone. It also helps our beautiful planet stay healthy. So, let's all be water-saving superheroes!

Let's Hope for More Rain!

This is a challenging time for many families in South Africa. We hope that the water pipes will be fixed very soon. We also hope that more rain will come to fill up the dams. Dams are like giant, giant pools that hold water for many homes and towns. And we hope that the police and other helpers can stop the criminal gangs from causing trouble. Everyone deserves clean, fresh water. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent, and its people are very resilient and strong! They often find clever ways to solve problems and help each other. We send our super big hugs to all the kids and families in South Africa who are going through this. Let's all remember to be grateful for the water we have and to use it wisely. Thank you for listening, super friends! See you next time for more news from Africa!
summary: Some children and families in South Africa are having trouble getting water from their taps, and bad groups of people are making it even harder by trying to steal or sell water for too much money.
whyMatters: Water is super important for everyone to drink, wash, and grow food, so it's a big problem when people don't have enough, and it's extra sad when bad people make things worse.
